L1.area: Native C extension layer and vendored library dependency management

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in a vendored XSLT processing library where a pointer variable is not properly reset after certain text copy operations involving CDATA sections in input documents. If the relevant memory area is freed and reused, a bounds check can fail, leading to out-of-bounds memory writes or disclosure of uninitialized data.

L3.area: Vendored libxslt dependency versioning and build patch configuration
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the vendored libxslt library's text copying routine where a cached text pointer is not properly reset when processing CDATA sections in input documents. This can lead to out-of-bounds memory writes or disclosure of uninitialized data when the referenced memory is freed and reused.

ground_truth.reason: In libxslt 1.1.33 (vendored by Nokogiri), the xsltCopyText function does not reset the ctxt->lasttext pointer under certain circumstances involving CDATA sections, leading to a use-after-free that can cause out-of-bounds writes or uninitialized data disclosure.
